title: aconitum japonicum subsp. ibukiense
intro: Aconitum japonicum subsp. ibukiense, also known as the Ibuki Monkshood, is a subspecies of the Aconitum japonicum plant, a perennial flowering plant native to Japan. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and analgesic effects due to the presence of aconitine, a potent alkaloid. The health benefits of Aconitum japonicum subsp. ibukiense include its ability to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
